fund.name: Nippon India Multi Cap Fund - Direct Plan Growth Plan - Growth Option
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund is a good mix of large, mid, and small-cap stocks, which helps balance growth and stability. It has a good track record and is suitable for a moderate risk appetite.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This fund focuses on large and mid-cap stocks, which are generally more stable than small-cap stocks. It is a good choice for a moderate risk appetite.

fund.name: Nippon India ETF Gold BeES
fund.fund_house: Nippon Life India Asset Management Limited

allocations.rationale: Gold is a safe investment that can protect against inflation. It is a good choice for a moderate risk appetite.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Kuvera, Groww, or MFU. Step 2: Start your monthly SIPs in these funds.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: check if your SIPs are running.
After 1 year: see if any fund has fallen behind compared to similar funds.
After 5 years: review your portfolio and consider rebalancing if needed.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if your mix has changed a lot. If one category has grown much more than planned, move some money to the others to get back to your target mix.
plan.rationale: Phoebe is 27 years old with a moderate risk appetite and a long investment horizon of 28 years. She is a freelance UX designer with a variable income, so she needs a flexible investment plan that allows her to top up when she is flush. The plan includes a mix of multi-cap, large & mid-cap, and gold funds to balance growth and stability. The funds chosen are suitable for her risk appetite and have a good track record. The plan is simple with only three funds, which fits her preference for a simple portfolio.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — your portfolio value will temporarily go down. This is normal and recovers over time.
Gold prices can fluctuate, and may not always keep up with inflation.
The funds chosen may not outperform other funds in their category.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase SIP by 10% every year to match salary growth

allocation_schedule.trigger: Start with a 85% equity and 15% gold allocation.
Reduce equity exposure and increase gold allocation to 30%.
Further reduce equity exposure and increase gold allocation to 40%.
Further reduce equity exposure and increase gold allocation to 50%.
Further reduce equity exposure and increase gold allocation to 60%.
Further reduce equity exposure and increase gold allocation to 70%.

aps.reasoning: The plan has no passive instruments in the equity allocation (all funds are actively managed). The turnover score is 0.3 because the plan recommends annual rebalancing but also includes a dynamic allocation schedule that changes over time. There is no emphasis on cost minimisation, and the plan relies on fund-specific research (track record, risk appetite) rather than cost. The time horizon is long-term, and the portfolio_activeness_score is 0.0 because all funds are actively managed with high alpha and tracking error, indicating active management.

pqs.reasoning: Goal alignment is high as the plan addresses emergency fund, medium-term goals, and long-term wealth with appropriate asset allocation. Diversification is moderate as it covers equity and gold but lacks debt instruments for risk mitigation. Risk return appropriateness is good for a moderate risk taker with a long horizon. Internal consistency is high as all elements align. Tax efficiency is neutral as the plan does not explicitly address tax implications for the new regime investor, though gold and equity funds are taxed at the investor's slab.
